columns.name: employee_id
columns.description: Unique employee identifier; primary key and merge key for sp_mysqltobq_load.sql incremental loads

columns.name: first_name
columns.description: Employee first name; sourced from varchar(50)

columns.name: last_name
columns.description: Employee last name; sourced from varchar(50)

columns.name: email
columns.description: Employee email address; sourced from varchar(100)

columns.name: phone_number
columns.description: Employee phone number; nullable; sourced from varchar(20)

columns.name: job_id
columns.description: Job role code identifier; sourced from varchar(20)

columns.name: salary
columns.description: Employee salary with two decimal places; sourced from decimal(8,2)

columns.name: commission_pct
columns.description: Commission percentage; nullable; sourced from decimal(4,2)

columns.name: manager_id
columns.description: Self-referencing FK to employee_id of the managing employee; NULL for top-level employees

columns.name: department_id
columns.description: Department identifier; nullable; cluster column for department-scoped analytics

columns.name: status
columns.description: Employment status; low-cardinality cluster column; sourced from varchar(10)

columns.name: created_date
columns.description: Record creation timestamp; stored in UTC

columns.name: updated_date
columns.description: Record last update timestamp; DAY partition column; second merge key for incremental loads via sp_mysqltobq_load.sql; NULL rows land in BigQuery __NULL__ partition

cardinality.notes: Many employees may report to one manager via manager_id → employee_id self-reference; top-level employees carry NULL manager_id
